programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: They Tried to Drill Baby Drill — We Stopped Them | Rosebank & Climate Action with Lauren MacDonald
programme.desc: When activists took on the Rosebank oil field — the largest undeveloped oil field in the UK — and won, the world took notice. In this episode of the Inspire Action Podcast, Ciara sits down with Lauren MacDonald — climate activist, Lead Campaigner at #StopRosebank, and fearless voice in the fight against fossil fuel expansion here in the UK. From the streets of Glasgow to London, Lauren has been on the frontlines of climate action for nearly a decade, drawing urgent attention to the direct link between fossil fuel extraction and the climate crisis. Tune in for an inspiring conversation about activism, courage, and what it really takes to move people to act.

programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: Netflix for Nature CEO | The Future of Impact Media - Sam Sutaria
programme.desc: Once heralded as ‘the Netflix for Nature’, free streaming service Waterbear has built a devoted fanbase of hundreds of thousands of subscribers looking to support documentaries that cover topics including social justice, climate change, regeneration, and more. Now, five years from its start, Waterbear is still offering a platform for documentary filmmakers to share their important work, while also looking to pivot into sharing various types of impact-driven media, and is breaking the mold on what digital storytelling should look like. Sam Sutaria, the company’s CEO, is leading the charge into a new era and sits down with us in the episode to share more about the company’s origins, successes, goals, and plans for the future.

programme.sub-title: 우리 산, 우리의 자연: 외설악의 울타리, 설악산 울산바위
programme.desc: 강원도 속초시와 고성군의 경계에 위치하며 6개의 봉우리가 병풍처럼 솟아 있고 둘레가 4km이르는 화강암으로 이루어진 거대한 바위산 이다. 울산바위라는 이름은 울타리같이 생겼다고 하여 ‘울산’이라는 설과 ‘우는 산’ 에서 유래했다는 설, 그리고 조물주가 금강산(金剛山)을 만들 때, 경상도 울산(蔚山) 지방의 바위가 금강산으로 찾아가다가 늦어서 여기에 자리 잡게 되어 울산바위라 한다는 전설이 전해지기도 한다.

programme.sub-title: Inside a Coastal House That Feels Settled, Not New
programme.desc: What does it mean for a house to tread lightly in a coastal landscape? At Point Lonsdale, Field Office Architects shaped a home that responds first to ecology, outlook and a sense of calm drawn from its surroundings. Organised around a north-facing orientation, the house flips the idea of a backyard. Outdoor living is brought to the front of the site, creating a generous, light-filled space that engages with both garden and street. Material restraint reinforces the architecture. Grey blockwork and timber detailing pick up the muted coastal tones, working together to provide thermal comfort, warmth and durability. Rather than seeking attention, the house offers a measured, enduring presence, one designed to support life as it unfolds.

programme.title: Simple Dwelling
programme.sub-title: What Does an ‘Enough’ House Look Like?
programme.desc: Can an architectural home be achieved on a tight budget? At Fisherman’s Walk in Barwon Heads, this coastal family house was shaped by restraint, where clarity, efficiency and first principles guided every decision. Set on a suburban flood-prone site, the home stretches east to west, prioritising northern light and a strong connection to the garden. Its simple form resists spectacle, offering instead a timeless presence that feels neither new nor old. Inside, warm, robust materials and a consistent structural grid bring order and calm. Recycled brick and Tasmanian oak balance economy with longevity, creating a home designed not to impress, but to be lived in. It is an architecture of enough.

programme.title: Simple Dwelling
programme.sub-title: Only What's Necessary with MGAO – Less, by Design
programme.desc: Is simplicity the same as minimalism? Matt Ross Goodman, Director of MGAO, reflects on an approach to simplicity defined by restraint rather than reduction. Grounded in the idea of as little as possible, and as much as necessary, he discusses the balance required to avoid over-simplification, and the role of context in shaping architecture that feels calm, practical and enduring. Through ideas of decluttering, Matt describes how simplicity can bring focus to what truly matters, allowing spaces to support daily life, rather than compete for attention.

programme.title: Simple Dwelling
programme.sub-title: Inside a Small Cabin Nestled in the Forest
programme.desc: What happens when a small cabin is designed to recede into the trees? Located within a low-lying forest in Bittern on the Mornington Peninsula, this compact retreat by Nic Morgante sits quietly beneath dense canopies, shaped as much by restraint as by the land itself. Approached along a gravel track, the cabin reveals itself gradually, raised lightly above the forest floor. A cantilevered verandah marks the edge of human intervention, beyond which the bush remains uninterrupted. Inside, all functions are pushed to the perimeter, allowing the central space to remain open and adaptable. Operable shutters, charred timber and a muted palette of timber and stainless steel enable the cabin to respond to light, weather and season, reconnecting daily life with the surrounding landscape.

programme.title: Simple Dwelling
programme.sub-title: This Architect’s Own Home Shows What 'Less but Better' Really Means
programme.desc: Can a “less but better” approach create a richer home? In Separation Creek, along the Great Ocean Road in Victoria, MGAO designed a 90m² retreat for his family where restraint and the essentials are not limitations, but the source of its richness. Embodying the spirit of the local Australian beach shacks, a simple elevated rectangle frames the ocean. Rational planning and cost-conscious detailing recapture the relaxed, unfenced character of the township. Internally, a muted palette and veranda-like glazing blur the threshold between interior and nature. Here, simplicity reconnects the act of living with the coastal landscape, the community and history that surrounds it.

programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: How Can We Reconnect with Nature? with Dr. Simon Sadinsky
programme.desc: What does it mean to be part of nature, not apart from it? And how can an ancient philosophy guide us toward practical, systemic change in the modern world? In this episode of the Inspire Action Podcast Ciara sits down with Dr. Simon Sadinsky - professor, Executive Director of Education, and in-house Harmony advisor at the Kings Foundation - to explore a philosophy that has guided cultures and peoples around the world for millennia, but is now finally being integrated into Western thought via a few key changemakers along the way. It shouldn’t be revolutionary, but it is in our world where progress often comes at the cost of the natural world around us. Simon reminds us that Harmony isn't just philosophy - it's a practical roadmap for action. This conversation will hopefully shift how you see your place in the world, and remind you (as it did for us) that we all have a part to play in creating the Harmony our planet so desperately needs.

programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: The Ugly Truth of Fast Fashion & The Joy of Buying Less with Aja Barber
programme.desc: What if we all decided to buy less and buy better? In this episode of the Inspire Action Podcast, we had the privilege of sitting down with fair-fashion campaigner, author, and thought-leader Aja Barber to learn about her own journey and career in the fashion and media industries, to touch on the real cost of fast fashion and overconsumption, and the joy that comes with buying less and buying better. In a world where we are taught to senselessly and unceasingly ‘BUY BUY BUY!’, overcoming overconsumption is as simple as: loving what we already have, fixing that which is broken, borrowing that which we only need for a short time, and seeking out secondhand as the first option when we want or need something new.

programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: Restore Nature & Bring Back Joy with Christabel Reed of Earthed
programme.desc: WE ALL NEED NATURE SKILLS. For millennia, knowledge and skillsets like tending land, saving seeds, maintaining soil health, and working within a community agricultural framework were passed down from generation to generation. In the midst of the industrial revolution, the creation of the 40-hour work week, and rapidly urbanising cities that don’t prioritise green spaces, these vital skills that connect us to the land are largely unknown to most in the West. In this episode of The Inspire Action Podcast, Ciara sits down with Christabel Reed, co-founder of platforms Advaya and Earthed. While Advaya centres around sharing knowledge and wisdom about mythology, ecology, and spirituality, Earthed offers free, online nature skills courses that aim to reconnect people with nature by getting them directly involved with it. Ciara and Christabel discuss ecological responsibility, bringing joy back to regeneration, land stewardship, barriers to entry, how our current systems can block our ability to be in nature as much as we need to be, and how everyone can work to restore our Earth.

programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: Super Seaweed Solving the Plastic Problem (with Pierre Paslier, Notpla)
programme.desc: SUPER SEAWEED TO THE RESCUE! No, this seaweed hasn’t been genetically modified to fight off mutants–it’s just working to combat the plastic crisis, of course! One of the most dire issues our world today faces is the overproduction and overconsumption of plastic. With waste streams ill-equipped to handle the volume and varying types of plastic, as much as 23 million metric tonnes of plastic end up in our world’s waterways each year! Not only is this plastic choking marine life and ecosystems, but it’s also having devastating impacts on human health, communities, and economies. Founded in 2014, Notpla is on a mission to eliminate single-use plastic packaging with their innovative seaweed-based product. Their seaweed packaging is entirely biodegradable, organic, and made from seaweed harvested for industrial use (no fishy habitat destruction here!) In this episode, Ciara and Pierre discuss the plastic crisis, Notpla’s incredible 2022 Earthshot Prize win, how their work is eliminating tens of millions of pieces of single-use plastic across Europe and the UK, their future ambitions to use their product to better our world.

programme.title: Seasonal Sister season 3

programme.desc: 북한산은 서울 북쪽에 위치한 산으로 산전체가 거대한 화강암으로 이루어져 있다. 국립공원으로 지정된 이 명산에는 여러 봉우리가 저마다의 자태를 뽐내고 있는데, 남쪽 능선에 자리한 비봉 정상에는 비석이 우뚝 솟아있다.

programme.title: Our mountain, Our nature: The beautiful landscape hidden in the fantastic rocks, Mt. Juwang Valley, Cheongsong
programme.sub-title: 우리 산, 우리의 자연: 외설악의 울타리, 설악산 울산바위
programme.desc: 강원도 속초시와 고성군의 경계에 위치하며 6개의 봉우리가 병풍처럼 솟아 있고 둘레가 4km이르는 화강암으로 이루어진 거대한 바위산 이다. 울산바위라는 이름은 울타리같이 생겼다고 하여 ‘울산’이라는 설과 ‘우는 산’ 에서 유래했다는 설, 그리고 조물주가 금강산(金剛山)을 만들 때, 경상도 울산(蔚山) 지방의 바위가 금강산으로 찾아가다가 늦어서 여기에 자리 잡게 되어 울산바위라 한다는 전설이 전해지기도 한다.

programme.title: Our mountain, Our nature: The beautiful landscape hidden in the fantastic rocks, Mt. Juwang Valley, Cheongsong

programme.title: The Inspire Action Podcast
programme.sub-title: The Man Flying Around The World In a Hydrogen Plane!
programme.desc: We sat down with Dr. Bertrand Piccard, the first man to fly around the world in a solar-powered plane, who is now setting his sights on completing the first-ever non-stop, zero-emission flight around the world powered by hydrogen! With skeptics and naysayers claiming it cannot be done, Dr. Piccard has worked tirelessly for years to both develop the technology capable of completing this trip, as well as shift how the world of aviation views making hydrogen-powered flight a reality. In this episode, we’ll gain insight into Dr. Piccard’s plans, how he hopes innovation can bring individuals from across the political spectrum together to create a greener future, and so much more.

programme.title: Going Green: Green Green World
programme.sub-title: The Winter Olympic Games Legacy | Milano Cortina 2026
programme.desc: The Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympic Games are transforming Northern Italy—and what they're leaving behind will last for generations. We spent a week on the ground exploring the Olympic legacy in action: an Olympic Village becoming homes for 1,700 students, mountain towns getting critical infrastructure upgrades, and accessibility improvements that will serve communities for decades. This isn't just about two weeks of competition—it's about building something that lasts.

programme.title: Going Green: Green Green World
programme.sub-title: The Paris Olympic Village: One Year On
programme.desc: We revisited the Olympic Village one year after this Paris 2024 Olympic Games. This is how the Olympic legacy has transformed an entire community! We've visited this Olympic Village twice before—once just before the games, and again right after they ended. Now, 1 year on, we're here to see the exciting reality: how this Olympic legacy is truly benefiting the local community. We spoke with the visionaries who built it, the experts studying its impact, and the locals who are using these incredible facilities every single day. From revolutionary sustainable design to genuine community transformation, there's a lot to cover so let's jump right in!

programme.title: Tg Del Turismo

Si raccontano percorsi come il Cammino dei Cappuccini e iniziative culturali in Sardegna legate alla tradizione francescana. Tra natura, spiritualità e comunità locali, emergono nuove forme di viaggio consapevole. Completano il quadro classifiche su mete più soleggiate ed economiche, suggerimenti di itinerari e eventi come la ...

programme.title: Going Green: Green Green World
programme.sub-title: Every Building in 2050 Will Be Covered in MOSS!
programme.desc: This is why moss is the future of sustainable architecture! Alive Labs in London, are growing moss panels that can be installed in cities to help remove air pollution, suck up carbon dioxide, regulate the temperature and absorb rainwater! Moss’ potential has not been fully harnessed in architecture until now. It’s fire-resistant due to the modular shape, requires very little maintenance and the shapes can be customised to create moss artwork! The surrounding material is also the world's first bio receptive concrete made from fully organic materials. Building facades offer so much opportunity to reintroduce biodiversity into cities. This is a practical, easy to install solution, which we hope to see in cities around the world soon.

programme.title: Island Cultural Heritage Journey

Un ritorno alla semplicità che richiama modelli del passato e conduce Syusy sulle tracce degli eremiti, maestri di uno stile di vita alternativo. Lungo il cammino, accompagnata dalla presenza simbolica di Dante Alighieri, Syusy attraversa il Mugello e l...

programme.title: Robin Lewis Makes
programme.sub-title: Mistakes Can Happen At Any Time On Site
programme.desc: This video documents my first-ever cabinet installation for a paying client — from cutting melamine sheets in the workshop to scribing fillers on site and figuring things out the hard way. I walk through the entire cabinetry process: breaking down materials, sizing parts, installing drawer hardware, fixing mistakes, and dealing with walls that are nowhere near plumb. If you’re interested in cabinet making, furniture building, or starting a small woodworking business, this project shows what the job actually looks like — not just the clean highlight reel. This was the last project of 2025 for me, and a big learning curve — especially around quoting install time, managing client expectations, and understanding where profit actually comes from in cabinetry work. If you’re growing a trade business, thinking about taking on installs, or just curious how cabinet makers price jobs, this one’s for you.

programme.title: Robin Lewis Makes
programme.sub-title: The Most Expensive Plywood I've Ever Worked With
programme.desc: I’m building a custom L-shaped shoe rack using premium Tasmanian Oak veneered plywood with a solid Victorian Ash seat. This project was a great client commission and a fun build, but with timber prices skyrocketing—and Victorian Ash becoming harder to source after the 2023 logging ban—it definitely pushed the limits of my margins. If you’re interested in furniture making, woodworking commissions, or want to see what it’s like working with high-end Tasmanian Oak plywood, this video walks through the full process: design, cutting, joinery, assembly, and installing the solid timber seat. I also talk about pricing custom furniture and how difficult Victorian Ash is becoming to find now that legislation has restricted harvesting.

programme.title: Robin Lewis Makes
programme.sub-title: Is The Laser Industry Evolving Quick Enough?
programme.desc: The laser engraving world is exploding — but has it evolved too fast for beginners to keep up? In this video, I dive into my experience setting up the Omtech Turbo 756 60W CO₂ laser, from the initial unboxing to the unexpected issues that exposed some growing pains in the laser industry. If you’re new to laser engraving, thinking about buying a CO₂ laser, or just curious about how the industry is shifting from industrial to residential spaces, this is a must-watch. I cover what worked, what didn’t, and why it feels like the laser engraving community is still catching up to the speed of its own innovation. The laser engraving industry is growing fast — but are we ready for it? Let’s find out.

programme.title: WE PLAY season 1

programme.title: Robin Lewis Makes
programme.sub-title: The Most Beautiful Desk I've Ever Built
programme.desc: Ever thought about making a desk out of end grain? In this video, I take the idea of end grain parquet flooring and flip it on its head — turning it into a custom end grain desk build made entirely from timber offcuts. I walk through the full process of milling, gluing, pattern design, and flattening — essentially creating an end grain board on a massive scale. Along the way, I dive into the big question every woodworker faces: can you glue wood that moves to something that doesn’t? From concerns about wood movement, moisture, and cracking, to experimenting with epoxy and creative inlay work, this project became a long-term experiment in how far you can push the rules of woodworking. Watch the full video to see how I turned a pile of offcuts into a functional, geometric end grain desk — and find out if it actually survived.

programme.title: El Amor No es Negocio
programme.desc: Dos hermanos de un pueblo rural llegan a la Gran Ciudad, donde conocen a un estafador seductor. Él se gana su confianza mientras busca aprovecharse de jóvenes ingenuas atraídas por la vida nocturna, como polillas a la llama.

programme.title: Memory Card: O Assassin’s Creed que não é Assassin’s Creed?
programme.desc: Piratas, falsificações e um jogo que pegou a fórmula de Assassin’s Creed, jogou no mar e voltou com uma das experiências mais insanas da franquia. Black Flag não é só sobre assassinos. É sobre pilantragem em alto-mar, trapaça e, claro, a liberdade digna da série. No terceiro episódio do Memory Card, a gente explora esse tesouro lançado em 2013, um jogo que provou que pra ser lendário, não precisa seguir as regras da irmandade. Edward Kenway não tem credo, não tem lealdade, só tem faro pra dinheiro e um navio pronto pra virar o caos. Vem relembrar essa pérola da Ubisoft, os altos e baixos da vida pirata e entender como Black Flag pirateou a própria série e virou referência máxima do gênero. Um episódio cheio de nostalgia, história e aquela certeza: se tem música de marujo, tem jogo bom.

programme.desc: Brian imagines his perfect life out loud; the perfect wife, the perfect kids, the two car garage, a white picket fence and even a golden retriever. It’s supposed to be an open and shut case, but when the victim’s brother, a former bounty hunter named Derrick arrives from America, the case begins to unravel. Convinced his sister was murdered and consumed by his lust for justice, Derrick embarks on a mission of revenge that threatens to tear apart the city of Lagos, while during the course of her investigation, Naji stumbles onto the trail of an unknown gun smuggler operating in the area. Naji’s case and Derrick’s lust for revenge come crashing together to reveal a crime of epic proportions and the two of them must team up together to save thousands of innocent lives.

programme.title: Inuyasha
programme.sub-title: S2E18 - La pericolosa spada di Kaijimbo
programme.desc: Raggiunto il luogo del combattimento tra Goshinki e Inuyasha, Sesshomaru recupera la testa del demone sconfitto e la porta da Kaijimbo, fabbro costruttore di spade un tempo ripudiato dal maestro Totosai a causa della sua propensione al male. L'intenzione di Sesshomaru è quella di ottenere una spada forgiata dalle zanne di Goshinki.

programme.title: La Promesa del Diablo
programme.desc: Cuando un egipcio felizmente casado y una viuda estadounidense son engañados por el diablo, con consecuencias devastadoras, unen fuerzas y se proponen

programme.title: The DOGTV Hour
programme.desc: A free daily hour of DOGTV’s expert-curated programming made to relax and enrich your dog’s day.

programme.title: Inuyasha
programme.desc: Completamente controllato dalla volontà della spada Tokijin, Kaijimbo sfida Inuyasha a duello, ma ben presto soccombe e muore, incapace di sopportare l'aura maligna della sua stessa spada. Nonostante la vittoria Inuyasha è ancora alle prese con l'impugnatura di Tessaiga, divenuta insopportabilmente pesante dopo la riparazione di Totosai.

programme.title: Inuyasha
programme.desc: Il gruppo di Inuyasha si è finalmente messo in cammino, quando viene raggiunto da Koga, il demone lupo innamorato di Kagome. Tra lui e il gelosissimo Inuyasha scoppia subito una lite che si conclude con la fuga di Koga.

programme.title: La Promesa del Diablo
programme.desc: Cuando un egipcio felizmente casado y una viuda estadounidense son engañados por el diablo, con consecuencias devastadoras, unen fuerzas y se proponen

programme.title: Pasiones Asesinas
programme.desc: La trama gira en torno a la muerte de Ana Mejías, una activista que es apuñalada por su marido, Leonardo Cabello.

programme.title: Inuyasha
programme.sub-title: S2E21 - Naraku e lo spirito di Onigumo
programme.desc: Inuyasha e Kagome litigano ancora per una sciocchezza, e mentre la ragazza se ne torna a casa (ma, come confida a Shippo, ha intenzione di assentarsi per poco: va solo a prendere bende e medicinali), Miroku e Sango se la prendono con Inuyasha.

programme.title: The Dog Moms
programme.desc: Chrissy and Amber explore aging, mindful walks, travel tips, and the value of pet insurance.

programme.title: Merging Traditional Finance with Blockchain Innovation | Alok Sinha from Pazalabs
programme.desc: Join us for an insightful sponsored episode of The Edge of Show as we welcome Alok Sinha, Founder and Chief Ecosystem Officer at Pazalabs. In this episode, we dive deep into the world of real-world assets (RWAs) and how blockchain technology is revolutionizing capital markets. Discover how they are addressing inefficiencies in loan origination, securitization, and liquidity through innovative solutions like AI automation and compliant fractionalization. We explore the complexities of creating a full lifecycle asset commerce ecosystem, the importance of risk and compliance, and how Pazalabs is bridging the gap between traditional finance (CeFi) and decentralized finance (DeFi).

programme.title: Inuyasha
programme.sub-title: S2E22 - Ritorno ai luoghi del primo incontro
programme.desc: Kagome, dopo aver assistito all'incontro tra Kikyo e Inuyasha, torna disperata nella sua epoca, dimenticando di lasciare nell'era Sengoku due frammenti della Sfera. Inizia per lei e Inuyasha una difficile separazione, che li aiuterà a far chiarezza nei loro sentimenti.
